description_long: This guard stone is a robust, elongated block with a rounded, bulbous end and a more squared-off tail. The surface is predominantly matte white, with subtle textural variations and some visible roughness, especially near the rounded head. The head features faint, smoothed indentations and possible chipping, suggesting repeated contact or weathering. The body is solid and heavy, with a consistent cross-section and no visible openings or separations. Its form is utilitarian, designed to act as a physical barrier to prevent vehicles from encroaching onto protected areas. The overall appearance is functional, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on durability.
description: A solid, elongated white guard stone with a rounded head and squared tail, showing rough, weathered surface details. Designed as a durable urban barrier with a utilitarian form.
description_view_0: The rounded head of the guard stone is prominent, with visible indentations and roughness, suggesting impact or wear.
description_view_1: The elongated body is most visible, showing a consistent, rectangular cross-section and a smooth, matte surface.
description_view_2: The squared-off tail is apparent, with straight edges and a flat end, contrasting with the rounded head.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the elongated body is shown, highlighting the overall length and the transition from the rounded head to the squared tail.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, at the entrance to driveways, or along the edge of sidewalks to protect buildings and pedestrian areas from vehicle intrusion.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be partially embedded in the ground with the rounded head facing outward, fully above ground but anchored for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and smoothing on the rounded head, likely caused by repeated vehicle contact and weathering. Some discoloration and erosion may be present on the upper surfaces due to exposure to the elements.
